idea.title: Updrift
idea.hook: Every mining game digs down — this one digs UP, and gravity is your enemy: carve a shaft toward the surface while loosened debris rains down on your head and your oxygen drains with every swing.
idea.prototype: Vertical strata grid; diver starts at the seabed. Each action — DIG-UP, BRACE, or BLAST — costs oxygen from a finite tank. Dug rock above can dislodge and fall a column; getting hit costs a crush-life. You auto-rise one tile when the cell above clears. Reach the surface row before oxygen empties. Controls: directional dig, brace, blast.
idea.mutation_of: reddit r/incremental_games 'Resurface — mining games make you dig down, why not up?' inversion
idea.why_novel: Inverts the dig-down genre so gravity opposes progress (falling debris) instead of aiding it. Core Drill (shipped) drills downward with no fall threat — flipping the gravity vector turns the same act into a hazard-management traversal puzzle.
idea.non_timer_pressure: Resource scarcity (oxygen drains per action) + crush lives
idea.lose_condition: Your oxygen tank empties before you reach the surface, or falling debris exhausts your crush-lives.

synthesizer_brief: market-radar 2026-05-23 daily-mix transfer_bet slot, physics_action family, danger_combat CTR archetype

alternatives.title: Unscrew
alternatives.hook: Back colored screws out of a junk sculpture in the right order before it collapses.

alternatives.title: Powder Route
alternatives.hook: Stamp scarce reagents then hit RUN and let falling-sand physics light every torch.

alternatives.title: Blind Sort
alternatives.hook: Order six cards when the sort rule is hidden.

alternatives.title: Backlot
alternatives.hook: Place flat facades so a rolling camera never catches a gap.

alternatives.title: Feed
alternatives.hook: Publish stories to steer a crowd to unity before fear riots.
